alignments.preprint_claim_text: AGMI is the first approach to explore gene constraint based multi-omics integration for drug response prediction using whole-genome data with Graph Neural Networks (GNNs). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AGMI is the first approach to explore gene constraint based multi-omics integration for drug response prediction using whole-genome data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the 'first approach' contribution and whole-genome focus but omits explicit mention of GNNs in the summary claim, slightly clarifying the contribution statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AGMI approach constructs a Multi-edge Graph (MeG) for each cell line to aggregate multi-omics features for drug response prediction. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication summary omits the specific methodological detail about constructing a Multi-edge Graph per cell line; that implementation detail does not appear among the publication claims and is therefore treated as removed from the top-level claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed neural network (NN) model can predict power flow results using historical power system data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed neural network (NN) model can predict power flow results using historical power system data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The texts are essentially identical in claim and certainty; only the statement type label differs, so the claim content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The NN-based power flow model can find solutions quickly and more accurately than the traditional DC power flow model. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The NN-based power flow model can find solutions quickly and more accurately than the traditional DC power flow model. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication make the same comparative performance claim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Data-driven methods can partially address inaccuracies in line flow results for some transmission lines. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Data-driven methods can partially address inaccuracies in line flow results for some transmission lines. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The claim text and level of certainty are the same; only the categorization label changes, so the claim content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accurate line flow results on critical lines are essential for power flow analysis due to the unpredictable nature of renewable energy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Accurate line flow results on critical lines are essential for power flow analysis due to the unpredictable nature of renewable energy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The statement is the same in substance and certainty; only the claim-type label differs between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of AI in environmental sciences can introduce unintended societal consequences similar to those seen in other domains. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of AI in environmental sciences can lead to unintended societal consequences similar to those observed in other domains. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Wording differs slightly but the substantive claim and certainty are the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI can hard code biases and increase inequalities, which are concerns that also apply to the environmental sciences. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI algorithms, despite being based on mathematical formulas, can introduce biases in environmental sciences. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the bias concern but drops the explicit claim about increasing inequalities; this is a narrowing/revision of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: With proper precautions, AI has the potential to reduce climate and environmental injustice. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: With proper precautions, AI has the potential to help reduce climate and environmental injustice. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim is substantively the same but the publication expresses higher certainty about AI's potential to reduce injustice.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The article aims to stimulate discussion and research efforts regarding the ethical use of AI in environmental sciences. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article aims to stimulate discussion and research efforts regarding the ethical use of AI in environmental sciences. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The aim and level of certainty remain the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Curriculum learning improves the generalizability of models for complex dynamical systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Curriculum learning can improve the generalization of models for complex dynamical systems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions assert the same effect of curriculum learning with high certainty; wording differences are minor.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The structure of the training set significantly impacts the quality of long-term predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The structure of the training set significantly impacts the quality of long-term predictions in data-driven modeling. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is the same; the publication adds a minor contextualization (in data-driven modeling) but does not change the substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Entropy can be used as a metric of complexity for the dataset, influencing the design of the training set. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Entropy can be used as a metric of complexity for the dataset, influencing the design of the training set. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The claim and certainty are consistent; only the claim-type label changes between versions.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A-priori guarantees of a faithful model of the physical system can be assessed by determining the sufficient amount of data. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: OptABC integrates multiple techniques, including K-Means clustering and opposition-based learning, to improve hyper-parameter tuning in machine learning models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: OptABC enhances the convergence ability of the ABC algorithm without significantly decreasing the accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the OptABC compared to existing approaches in the literature. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of OptABC compared to existing state-of-the-art approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both assert experimental superiority/effectiveness, with the publication specifying 'state-of-the-art' but not changing the substantive result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is broad variation in how densely languages encode information into their words across a sample of ~1,000 languages. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is broad variation in how densely languages encode information into words across a sample of ~1,000 languages. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is essentially identical in content and certainty between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Language information density is associated with a denser configuration of semantic information. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Language information density is associated with a denser configuration of semantic information. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Informationally denser languages tend toward faster communication. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Informationally denser languages tend towards faster communication but conceptually narrower conversations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Informationally denser languages tend toward conceptually narrower conversations within which topics of conversation are discussed at greater depth. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Informationally denser languages tend towards faster communication but conceptually narrower conversations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The structure of language shapes the nature and texture of human engagement, with consequences for human behavior across levels of society. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The structure of language shapes the nature and texture of human engagement, with consequences for human behaviour across levels of society. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The broader sociocultural claim is the same in both documents with matching certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The context of biased words in different news outlets varies more strongly than that of non-biased words.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hypothesis that a word’s representations in both word embedding spaces are more similar for non-biased words than biased words was not statistically significant.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The results show the effectiveness of the approach, with 31% of the words with the largest distances potentially inducing bias.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A larger dataset is needed to improve the results of the study.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset needs to be significantly larger to improve the results of the study.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the original limitation by specifying the dataset must be "significantly larger," clarifying and amplifying the recommendation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper presents the first in-depth look at the context of bias words measured by word embeddings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper presents the first in-depth look at the context of bias words measured by word embeddings.
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is carried over verbatim from preprint to publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Porosity has been identified as the key indicator of the durability properties of concrete exposed to aggressive environments.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state or support that porosity itself is the key indicator of durability under aggressive environments, so this claim was not retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The regression tree ensembles can accurately predict the porosity of concrete from its mixture compositions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed data-driven approach achieves a higher prediction accuracy of R² = 0.9770, MAPE = 2.97%, and RMSE = 0.431 for porosity (%).

alignments.pub_claim_text: Gradient boosting trees outperform random forests in terms of their prediction accuracy for concrete porosity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The out-of-bag error based hyperparameter tuning strategy for random forests is found to be much more efficient than k-Fold Cross-Validation.

alignments.pub_claim_text: XGBoost achieves the best performance with additional regularization over model complexity to prevent overfitting.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Curing days, water/binder ratio, and aggregate content are the most important predictors of concrete porosity.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The SCORE framework incorporates second-order information in the Newton-decrement framework for convex optimization.

alignments.pub_claim_text: GGN-SCORE demonstrates how to speed up convergence while also improving model generalization for regularized minimization problems.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical experiments show that GGN-SCORE is efficient and converges faster compared to baseline first-order and quasi-Newton methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical experiments show that GGN-SCORE has fast convergence compared to baseline first-order and quasi-Newton methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method is promising for non-convex optimization problems, as shown by additional experiments involving non-convex neural network training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method is promising for non-convex optimization problems, as shown in additional experiments involving overparameterized neural network training.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The GGN-SCORE algorithm reduces computational overhead by exploiting the structure of the second-order information in the Hessian matrix.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduced instrument and evaluation method for optical bubble stream characterization can effectively target flows of up to 100 ml/min and bubbles with a few millimeters radius.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The dedicated instrument employs a high-speed deep sea capable stereo camera system that can record terabytes of bubble imagery for later automated analysis.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieves better performance over existing methods in terms of generalization ability and quality of details. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Factored PCA (FPCA) is susceptible to outliers due to its Gaussian assumption.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed robust extension of FPCA (RFPCA) utilizes a matrix-variate $t$ distribution to improve robustness against outliers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RFPCA can adaptively down-weight outliers and yield robust estimates.

alignments.rationale: Both claims refer to RFPCA's down-weighting/weighting mechanism for outlier handling; the publication frames this as expected latent weights useful for detection, a clearer operationalization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that RFPCA performs favorably compared to several related methods for matrix-valued outlier detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: RFPCA is compared favorably with several closely related methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a non-exhaustive set of nine real world challenges for reinforcement learning control in grid-interactive buildings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework of nine real world challenges for RL control in grid-interactive buildings (GIBs) is essential for standardizing research in this area.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution as "essential for standardizing research," increasing the claim's asserted importance and broadening its claimed impact beyond the preprint's descriptive phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sequence of operations utilized in a rule based controller (RBC) used for offline training affects the performance of the RL agents when evaluated on a set of four energy flexibility metrics.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include a corresponding claim about the effect of RBC operation sequence; that specific assertion is absent in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Longer offline learning from an optimized RBC leads to improved performance in the long run.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Longer offline training from an optimized rule based controller (RBC) leads to improved performance of RL agents in the long run.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same result claim with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RL agents that learn from a simplified RBC risk poorer performance as the offline learning period increases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: RL agents that train on logs from a simplified RBC risk poorer performance as the offline training period increases.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same cautionary result about training on simplified RBC logs with equivalent scope and expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We observe no impact on performance from information sharing amongst agents.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is no impact on performance from information sharing amongst agents in the context of RL control.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the null finding about information sharing with the same scope and level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed adaptive structure learning method effectively regularizes the cooperation of semi-supervised learning and domain adaptation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed adaptive structure learning method effectively regularizes the cooperation of semi-supervised learning and domain adaptation.
alignments.rationale: Claim is identical between preprint and publication in wording and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework improves intra-class density and enhances the smoothness of the decision boundary through the use of two classifier networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework improves intra-class density and enhances the smoothness of the decision boundary through the use of two classifier networks.
alignments.rationale: The result claim is repeated verbatim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art approaches on standard SSDA benchmarks, including DomainNet and Office-home.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art approaches on standard SSDA benchmarks, including DomainNet and Office-home.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same experimental claim with identical scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Surrogate compilation accelerates the CPU simulator under study by 1.6 times.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Surrogate compilation accelerates the CPU simulator under study by 1.6×.
alignments.rationale: The numeric acceleration claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Surrogate adaptation decreases the simulator's error by up to 50%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Surrogate adaptation decreases the simulator’s error by up to 50%.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantitative error-reduction claim without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Surrogate optimization finds simulation parameters that decrease the simulator's error by 5% compared to the error induced by expert-set parameters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Surrogate optimization finds simulation parameters that decrease the simulator’s error by 5% compared to the error induced by expert-set parameters.
alignments.rationale: The claim about parameter-finding and the 5% improvement is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper formalizes a taxonomy of surrogate-based design patterns.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper formalizes a taxonomy of surrogate-based design patterns.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim about formalizing a taxonomy is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work builds a foundation for workflows based on programming with surrogates of programs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work builds a foundation for workflows based on programming with surrogates of programs.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the same foundational/conceptual contribution without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The periodic residual learning network (PRNet) effectively models periodicity in crowd flow data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel periodic residual learning network (PRNet) effectively models periodicity in crowd flow data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution claim with equivalent wording and certainty, so the meaning and scope are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Learning the stationary deviation between inputs and outputs facilitates model training compared to directly predicting highly dynamic crowd flows. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Learning the stationary deviation between inputs and outputs facilitates model training compared to directly predicting highly dynamic crowd flows. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The textual claim is identical; only the label (conclusion/result) differs, but the substantive claim, scope, and confidence are preserved.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned variation from PRNet enables the network to produce more accurate multi-step ahead predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result claim with equivalent meaning and certainty, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PRNet can be easily integrated into existing models to enhance their predictive performance. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PRNet can be easily integrated into existing models to enhance their predictive performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim about easy integration is preserved verbatim; only the claim-type label changed, so semantics, scope, and confidence remain the same.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Micro-expressions are useful in inferring a person's true emotions despite attempts to conceal them. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: We introduce the first method that is able to reconstruct photorealistic render-ready 3D facial geometry and BRDF from a single 'in-the-wild' image. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed attribute-shaped learning (ASL) framework can jointly perform query attributes generation and discriminative visual representation learning for few-shot recognition. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed attribute-shaped learning (ASL) framework can jointly perform query attributes generation and discriminative visual representation learning for few-shot recognition. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The visual-attribute predictor (VAP) is constructed to predict the attributes of queries. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The attribute-visual attention module (AVAM) is designed to adaptively utilize attributes and visual representations to learn more discriminative features. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The equivalent diameter of myelinated axons in somatosensory cortex was decreased in TBI animals five months after the injury. (certainty=low, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Supervised learning allows us to identify weighted-P4s which admit Calabi-Yau hypersurfaces to 100% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach generates images with visual quality on par with state-of-the-art methods despite only using data from a short time interval. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach generates images with visual quality on par with state-of-the-art methods despite only using data from a short time interval. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The formulation can be naturally combined with super-resolution, motion-segmentation, and color demosaicing. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.rationale: The identification of two leakages is present in both; the publication frames it as a limitation but the substantive claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed solution can be extended to tree ensemble models such as random forest and gradient boosting decision tree. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the preprint claim about extension to ensemble models, so this claim appears to have been omitted in the final paper.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Theoretical and experimental analysis suggest that Pivot is efficient for the privacy achieved. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This efficiency claim about theoretical and experimental analysis appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our proposed method leveraging the use of cortical and subcortical surface information outperforms other machine learning methods with a 96.35% testing accuracy for the ADD vs. healthy control problem. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our proposed method leveraging the use of cortical and subcortical surface information outperforms other machine learning methods with a 96.35% testing accuracy for the ADD vs. healthy control problem. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is carried over verbatim into the publication without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a residual learning framework for state-of-the-art graph convolutional networks which offers a significant reduction in learnable parameters. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a residual learning framework for state-of-the-art graph convolutional networks which offers a significant reduction in learnable parameters. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution about residual learning framework and parameter reduction is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generated visualization maps in our study show correspondences with current knowledge regarding the structural localization of pathological changes in the brain associated with dementia of the Alzheimer's type. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generated visualization maps in our study show correspondences with current knowledge regarding the structural localization of pathological changes in the brain associated with dementia of the Alzheimer’s type. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The visualization correspondence claim appears in both versions with equivalent meaning (minor typographic difference only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our work offers visual interpretability of the network via class-specific gradient information that localizes important regions of interest in our inputs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not contain the explicit claim about class-specific gradient-based interpretability, indicating this preprint claim was omitted in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: C-arm Cone-beam CT (CBCT) provides intraoperative 3D tomographic imaging that allows for immediate verification and revision of screw placement.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim restates this specific clinical/operational statement about screw placement or immediate verification; the paper focuses on method/results rather than this background claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reconstruction quality attainable with commercial CBCT devices is insufficient due to severe metal artifacts caused by a mismatch in image formation physics.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly restate this limitation claim about commercial CBCT devices; it frames the work as improving imaging but does not reproduce this exact diagnostic assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adjusting the CBCT trajectory to acquire recommended views results in non-circular source orbits that avoid poor images and data inconsistencies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method represents a step toward online patient-specific C-arm CBCT source trajectories that enable high-quality tomographic imaging in the operating room.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the specific finding about non-circular orbits into a higher-level statement about patient-specific source trajectories enabling better imaging, broadening the emphasis from a specific orbit result to an overall capability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks trained on realistically simulated data can predict quality metrics that enable scene-specific adjustments of the CBCT source trajectory.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks trained on realistically simulated data can predict quality metrics that enable scene-specific adjustments of the CBCT source trajectory.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result essentially verbatim, with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tomographic reconstructions from scene-specific CBCT acquisitions exhibit improved image quality, particularly in terms of metal artifacts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tomographic reconstructions of scene-specific CBCT acquisitions exhibit improved image quality, particularly in terms of metal artifacts.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this reconstruction-quality improvement claim directly and with equivalent emphasis on metal-artifact reduction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel uplift modeling technique introduced in this paper dynamically optimizes the incremental treatment outcome while satisfying Return on Investment (ROI) constraints.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel uplift modeling technique introduced in this paper dynamically optimizes the incremental treatment’s outcome while satisfying Return on Investment (ROI) constraints.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this core contribution verbatim with the same framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The technique leverages Retrospective Estimation to address training data bias, long term effects, and seasonality challenges.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The technique leverages Retrospective Estimation to address training data bias, long term effects, and seasonality challenges.
alignments.rationale: This methodological point is carried over into the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Testing of the method via offline experiments and online randomized controlled trials at Booking.com resulted in a significant increase in the target outcome.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method was tested at Booking.com, resulting in a significant increase in the target outcome while remaining within financial constraints.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the main empirical result (significant increase) from Booking.com; it adds mention of financial constraints but maintains the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach outperformed other approaches while remaining within the required financial constraints.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach outperformed other methods in the context of online travel platforms.
alignments.rationale: The publication separates outperforming from the financial-constraint statement (the latter appears with Q2), and reports the outperforming result with somewhat lower certainty/context (medium), so the claim is slightly tempered and reframed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed distillation-based semi-supervised FL (DS-FL) algorithm reduces communication costs by up to 99% compared to traditional federated learning benchmarks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed distillation-based semi-supervised federated learning (DS-FL) algorithm reduces communication costs by up to 99 percent compared to traditional federated learning benchmarks.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this quantitative communication-cost reduction claim directly and without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DS-FL enhances model performance through data augmentation by creating an additionally labeled dataset from the outputs of local models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model performance is enhanced due to the data augmentation effect from the additionally labeled dataset created by the exchanged model outputs.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries over the same mechanism and effect wording about performance gains via additionally labeled data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The heterogeneity of the devices' dataset in DS-FL leads to ambiguity in each data sample and slows down training convergence.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The heterogeneity of the devices’ dataset in DS-FL leads to ambiguity in each data sample and slows down training convergence.
alignments.rationale: This limitation is reproduced in the publication with the same content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Entropy reduction averaging is proposed to sharpen aggregated model outputs and mitigate the effects of dataset heterogeneity in DS-FL.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Entropy reduction averaging is proposed to sharpen aggregated model outputs and mitigate the effects of dataset heterogeneity.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes the same proposed mitigation technique and rationale.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed multi-mask self-supervised learning via data undersampling (SSDU) outperforms SSDU and performs closely with supervised DL-MRI in knee MRI reconstruction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed multi-mask self-supervised learning via data undersampling (SSDU) outperforms single-mask SSDU and performs as well as supervised DL-MRI in knee MRI reconstruction.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the wording from 'performs closely' to 'performs as well as' supervised DL-MRI, amplifying the confidence in equivalence while keeping the core claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A clinical reader study ranks the multi-mask SSDU higher than supervised DL-MRI in terms of SNR and aliasing artifacts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A clinical reader study ranks the multi-mask SSDU higher than supervised DL-MRI in terms of signal-to-noise ratio and aliasing artifacts.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this reader-study ranking with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multi-mask SSDU achieves better reconstruction quality compared to SSDU in brain MRI.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multi-mask SSDU achieves better reconstruction quality compared with SSDU in brain MRI.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries this brain-MRI reconstruction improvement claim forward unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reader study demonstrates that multi-mask SSDU at R=8 significantly improves reconstruction compared to single-mask SSDU at R=8 and CG-SENSE at R=2.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multi-mask SSDU at R = 8 significantly improves reconstruction compared with single-mask SSDU at R = 8 and CG-SENSE at R = 2.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this specific reader-study comparison and significance claim intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed adversarial framework generates filtered images that mislead classifiers by considering both image content and label semantics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework generates filtered images with a high success rate.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes high success-rate outcome; the preprint additionally highlighted the role of label semantics and content—the publication separates outcome from mechanism, so the original mechanistic emphasis is clarified into a result-focused statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The structure loss in the framework defines the type and magnitude of perturbations based on a target image processing filter.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The structure loss helps generate perturbations whose type and magnitude are defined by a target image processing filter.
alignments.rationale: This contribution about the structure loss is carried into the publication with equivalent wording and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The semantic adversarial loss prevents the filtered image from being classified with a label in the same group as the target labels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The semantic adversarial loss considers groups of semantic labels to craft perturbations.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same idea about grouping of semantic labels for the adversarial loss; the difference is mostly wording, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework demonstrates a high success rate, robustness, and transferability of adversarially filtered images to unseen classifiers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework demonstrates robustness and transferability to unseen classifiers.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly highlights robustness and transferability; the high success-rate component is reported separately (Q0), so the composite preprint claim is split/clarified across publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number of hidden neurons up to the dimension number of a problem space is sufficient for the performance of shallow neural networks (SNNs). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The number of hidden neurons of a shallow neural network (SNN) depends on the nonlinearity of the training data, and the number of hidden neurons up to the dimension number of a problem space could be enough. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint claim but adds a condition that dependence on data nonlinearity matters, clarifying and narrowing the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learnability of SNNs produced by KPI-PDBP is better than that of SNNs optimized by KPI-VPSO in terms of performance and learning time on training data sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learnability of SNNs, produced by error-driven PDBP, is always better than that of SNNs, optimized by error-driven VPSOs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the preprint's dataset-limited claim into an absolute ('always') statement and omits the explicit mention of learning time, so the claim is revised and broadened with increased confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The robustness of SNNs learned by KPI-PDBPs and KPI-VPSOs is dependent on the data sets used. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim corresponds to the dataset-dependent robustness conclusion; the publication does not report this point.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ACC-PDBPs outperform other classic machine learning models across almost all tested data sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Comparing with other classic machine learning algorithms, such as C4.5, NB and NN in literature, the SNNs, obtained by accuracy-driven PDBPs, win for all tested data sets, and the improvement percentage is up to 32.86%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a stronger result (wins for all tested sets and quantifies improvement) relative to the preprint's 'almost all' statement, thus revising and amplifying the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automated capture of animal pose is transforming how we study neuroscience and social behavior. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automated capture of animal pose is transforming how we study neuroscience and social behavior. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Current methods are not able to robustly estimate pose and shape of animals, particularly for social animals such as birds. (certainty=high, type=limitation)

alignments.pub_claim_text: We introduce a model and multi-view optimization approach to capture the unique shape and pose space displayed by live birds. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: We provide extensive multi-view keypoint and mask annotations collected from a group of 15 social birds housed together in an outdoor aviary. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep-learning architecture predicts the outcome of chronic disease drug prescription using a graph kernel. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach outperforms current state-of-the-art models in terms of accuracy and interpretability. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DAEMON is platform-agnostic, as it achieved accurate classification results on two large-scale datasets of malicious Android applications without any algorithmic change. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DAEMON obtained highly accurate classification results on multiple datasets, establishing its dataset-agnostic and platform-agnostic capabilities. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The PITT-ConvLSTM model can successfully forecast the volumetric velocity of the Loop Current and its eddies for a period greater than 1 week. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Trove is a framework for weakly supervised entity classification using medical ontologies and expert-generated rules. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Trove offers performance comparable to learning from manually labeled training data. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Trove's approach is easy to share and modify compared to hand-labeled notes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of multi-scale images as input enhances the performance of the discriminator in the proposed method. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The multi-scale input / discriminator performance claim is present in the publication but not in the preprint, so it is an added result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates the feasibility of automatically generating alerts for problematic antibodies by extracting statements about antibody specificity from the literature. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study addresses the challenge of antibody specificity by proposing an automated alert system for problematic antibodies. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed deep neural network system can accurately classify snippets about antibody specificity and link them to specific antibodies, achieving weighted F-scores over 0.925 and 0.923, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed deep neural network system can accurately classify snippets about antibody specificity with a weighted F1-score of 0.925. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the classification F1 (0.925) but does not present the linking F-score as stated in the preprint, effectively narrowing the claim to classification and changing reported linking metrics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall performance of the system in completing the joint task of classification and linking is 0.914. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the single overall joint-task score of 0.914 present in the preprint, so that specific aggregate result appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research indicates that leveraging Research Resource Identifiers (RRID) allows for precise identification of antibodies linked to extracted specificity snippets. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system achieved an accuracy of 0.962 in linking snippets to specific antibodies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives a high numeric linking accuracy (0.962), which clarifies and strengthens the preprint's claim that RRIDs enable precise identification.

alignments.pub_claim_text: It is feasible to construct a reliable knowledge base about problematic antibodies through text mining. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GloDyNE can achieve superior or comparable performance compared to state-of-the-art Dynamic Network Embedding methods in three typical downstream tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GloDyNE can achieve superior or comparable performance compared to state-of-the-art Dynamic Network Embedding methods in three typical downstream tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed node selecting strategy in GloDyNE effectively preserves the global topology of a dynamic network. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Differential privacy can be used to improve security in AI applications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Differential privacy can be used to improve security, stabilize learning, build fair models, and impose composition in selected areas of AI. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Differential privacy can stabilize learning processes in AI. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Differential privacy can be used to improve security, stabilize learning, build fair models, and impose composition in selected areas of AI. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Differential privacy can help build fair models in AI. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Differential privacy can be used to improve security, stabilize learning, build fair models, and impose composition in selected areas of AI. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The fairness-related claim appears as part of the publication's combined result statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Differential privacy imposes composition in selected areas of AI. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Differential privacy can be used to improve security, stabilize learning, build fair models, and impose composition in selected areas of AI. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly lists composition among the attractive DP properties, so the claim is preserved within the aggregated result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No study has documented which differential privacy mechanisms can be leveraged to overcome issues in AI. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: No study has documented which differential privacy mechanisms can or have been leveraged to overcome issues in AI. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the limitation claim but strengthens its certainty and clarifies the wording that no study has documented mechanisms, increasing the stated confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Differential privacy has several attractive properties that can help solve problems related to privacy violations, security issues, and model fairness in AI. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This more general concluding statement about DP's attractive properties is present in the publication but not stated in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This article delivers a new view on many possibilities for improving AI performance with differential privacy techniques. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit contribution statement about presenting a new view on DP possibilities that does not appear in the preprint claims list.
